title: Racinaea spiculosa
taxonomy: Racinaea spiculosa is a species of bromeliad, which is a type of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Bromeliaceae, which is a large and diverse group of epiphytes and terrestrial plants. Within the Bromeliaceae family, Racinaea spiculosa is classified as a member of the subfamily Tillandsioideae. This subfamily is further divided into several genera, with Racinaea being one of them. The specific classification of Racinaea spiculosa is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, order Poales, family Bromeliaceae, subfamily Tillandsioideae, genus Racinaea, species R. spiculosa.

intro: Racinaea spiculosa, also known as the 'Tongue of Fire' or 'Puya', is a type of bromeliad, a flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Racinaea spiculosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Racinaea spiculosa is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, colds, and flu.
